conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 and lives at home with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few questions about his circumstances.

Since Alex is 18, his eligibility depends on whether he's in approved education or training. Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is currently studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. A-level studies at sixth form college would typically count as approved education.

To confirm eligibility, I need to know: when did Alex turn 18, or when will he turn 18?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He turned 18 a few months ago, but he's still in his A-level course - he hasn't finished yet.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. A few more questions to complete the assessment:

1. First, are you the person responsible for Alex - meaning do you live with him and are you responsible for his upbringing and care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives here at home with me and I'm responsible for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions:

Do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One final question to complete my assessment:

Is there anyone else who is also claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or are you the only person claiming (or intending to claim) for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm the only one. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 18 and still in full-time approved education (studying for his A-levels at sixth form college)
- Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training
- You are responsible for Alex and he lives with you in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

Since Alex is still completing his A-level course, you can continue to receive Child Benefit for him. The benefit will typically continue until he finishes his course or turns 20, whichever comes first (as long as he remains in approved education).

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit entitlement?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. He is 18 years old and currently studying for his A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as full-time non-advanced education. Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training. Alex turned 18 a few months ago but has not yet completed his A-level course, so he still qualifies. The claimant is responsible for Alex, lives with him in the UK (Manchester), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Alex meets the age extension criteria as he is still in full-time approved education (A-levels at sixth form). The claimant is responsible for Alex, they live together in the UK, and no one else is claiming for him.

Alex turned 18 a few months ago but has not yet completed his A-level course, so he still qualifies. The claimant is responsible for Alex, lives with him in the UK (Manchester), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.

Alex meets the age extension criteria as he is still in full-time approved education (A-levels at sixth form). The claimant is responsible for Alex, they live together in the UK, and no one else is claiming for him.
